返回本批次

双子座机器人-ER 1.6

Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6

deepmind.google 作者 markerbrod 分数 207 评论 69 2026/04/15 22:02
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 2026 年 4 月 14 日 模特劳拉·格雷瑟 (Laura Graesser) 和徐鹏 (Peng Xu) 为了使机器人真正为我们的日常生活和工业提供帮助，它们必须做的不仅仅是遵循指令，它们还必须对物理世界进行推理。从复杂的设施导航到解释压力表上的指针，机器人的“具体推理”使其能够弥合数字智能和身体动作之间的差距。今天，我们推出 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6，这是对我们的推理优先模型的重大升级，使机器人能够以前所未有的精度了解其环境。通过增强空间推理和多视图理解，我们正在为下一代物理代理带来新的自主水平。该模型专门研究对机器人技术至关重要的推理能力，包括视觉和空间理解、任务规划和成功检测。它充当机器人的高级推理模型，能够通过本机调用 Google 搜索等工具来查找信息、视觉语言动作模型 (VLA) 或任何其他第三方用户定义的函数来执行任务。Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 比 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 和 Gemini 3.0 Flash 都有显着改进，特别增强了空间和物理推理功能，例如指向、计数和成功检测。我们还解锁了一项新功能：仪器读取，使机器人能够读取复杂的仪表和观察镜——这是我们通过与合作伙伴波士顿动力公司密切合作发现的一个用例。从今天开始，Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 可通过 Gemini API 和 Google AI Studio 向开发人员开放。为了帮助您入门，我们正在分享一个开发人员 Colab，其中包含如何配置模型并提示其执行具体推理任务的示例。图 1：比较 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 与 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 和 Gemini 3.0 Flash 模型的基准结果。仪器读数评估是在启用代理视觉的情况下运行的（Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 除外，它不支持它）。所有其他评估均在禁用代理视觉的情况下进行。单视图和多视图成功检测评估包含不同的示例，因此不具有可比性。 指向：空间推理的基础 指向是体现推理模型的基本功能，随着每个模型的生成而不断发展。点可以用来表达许多概念，包括：空间推理：精确的对象检测和计数关系逻辑：进行比较，例如识别集合中最小的项目；定义“从到”关系（例如，将 X 移动到位置 Y）运动推理：映射轨迹并识别最佳抓取点约束合规性：通过复杂的提示进行推理，例如“指向每个小到足以放入蓝色杯子内的物体”Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 可以使用点作为中间步骤来推理更复杂的任务。例如，它可以使用点来计算图像中的项目，或识别图像上的显着点，以帮助模型执行数学运算以改进其度量估计。下面的示例显示了 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 在指向多个元素以及知道何时和何时不指向方面的优势。Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 正确识别了锤子 (2)、剪刀 (1)、画笔 (1)、钳子 (6)、以及花园工具的集合，可以解释为单个组或多个点。它并不指向图像中不存在的请求物品——独轮车和 Ryobi 钻头。相比之下，Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 无法识别锤子或油漆刷的正确数量，完全错过剪刀，幻觉独轮手推车，并且钳子指向缺乏精确性。 Gemini 3.0 Flash 与 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 接近，但不能处理钳子。 成功检测：自主引擎在机器人技术中，了解任务何时完成与了解如何开始它同样重要。成功检测是自主的基石，作为关键的决策引擎，允许智能体在重试失败的尝试或进入计划的下一阶段之间进行智能选择。在机器人中实现视觉理解具有挑战性，需要复杂的感知和推理能力与广泛的世界知识相结合，以便处理复杂的因素，例如遮挡、光线不足和模糊的指令。此外，大多数现代机器人设置都包括多个摄像头视图，例如头顶和手腕上的摄像头。这意味着系统需要了解不同视点如何在每个时刻和跨时间组合形成连贯的图片。Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 推进了多视图推理，使系统能够更好地理解多个摄像机流及其之间的关系，即使在动态或遮挡环境中也是如此，如下面典型的多视图场景所示。Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 汲取线索从多个摄像头视图中确定“将蓝色笔放入黑色笔架”的任务何时完成。 仪器读取：现实世界视觉推理要了解 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 的关键优势，我们必须了解它如何结合空间推理和世界知识等功能来解决复杂的现实世界问题。一个完美的例子是仪器读数。这项任务源于设施检查需求，这是我们波士顿动力合作伙伴的一个关键关注领域。工业设施包含许多仪器——温度计、压力表、化学观察镜等等——需要持续监控。波士顿动力公司的机器人产品 Spot 能够访问整个设施中的仪器并捕获它们的图像。Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 使机器人能够解释各种仪器，包括圆形压力计、垂直水平指示器和现代数字读数。仪器读取需要复杂的视觉推理。人们必须精确感知各种输入——包括针头、液位、容器边界、刻度线等——并理解它们之间的相互关系。对于视镜，这涉及到估计液体填充视镜的量，同时考虑到相机视角的畸变。仪表通常有描述单位的文本，必须读取和解释，有些仪表有多个指针，指的是需要组合的不同小数位。仪表读数和更可靠的任务推理等功能将使 Spot 能够完全自主地看到、理解和应对现实世界的挑战。波士顿动力 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 的 Spot 副总裁兼总经理 Marco da Silva 通过使用将视觉推理与代码执行相结合的代理视觉来实现高度准确的仪器读数。该模型采取中间步骤：首先放大图像以更好地读取仪表中的小细节，然后使用指向和代码执行来估计比例和间隔并获得准确的读数，最后应用其世界知识来解释含义。图 2：Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 的不同元素如何有助于在仪器读取任务上达到高水平的性能。准确读取模拟仪表此示例演示了模型如何使用指向和代码执行进行缩放以将仪表读数导出到小刻度准确性。我们迄今为止最安全的机器人模型安全性已集成到我们具体推理模型的各个级别中。 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 是我们迄今为止最安全的机器人模型，与前几代产品相比，在对抗性空间推理任务上表现出对 Gemini 安全政策的卓越合规性。该模型还显示出遵守物理安全约束的能力得到了显着提高。例如，它通过空间输出做出更安全的决策，例如指出哪些物体可以在夹具或材料限制下安全操作（例如，“不要处理液体”、“不要拾起超过 20 公斤的物体”）。我们还测试了该模型根据现实生活中的伤害报告在文本和视频场景中识别安全隐患的能力。在这些任务中，我们的 Gemini Robotics-ER 模型在准确感知伤害风险方面比基线 Gemini 3.0 Flash 性能有所提高（文本 +6%，视频 +10%）。图 3：与 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 相比，Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 在安全指令方面有了显着改进，接下来测试遵守物理安全约束的能力。与 Gemini 3.0 Flash 相比，它在指向方面有所改进，并且两种型号都具有非常高的文本准确度。 Gemini 3.0 Flash 在边界框方面​​做得更好。与我们合作改进机器人的具体推理我们致力于确保 Gemini Robotics-ER 为机器人社区提供最大价值。如果您的专业应用当前的能力有限，我们邀请您提交此表格，其中包含 10-50 张带标签的图像，说明特定的故障模式，以帮助我们构建更强大的推理功能。我们期待与您合作，在即将发布的版本中增强这些功能。立即在 Google AI Studio 上试用 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6

Original Summary

  1. April 14, 2026 ModelsLaura Graesser and Peng XuFor robots to be truly helpful in our daily lives and industries, they must do more than follow instructions, they must reason about the physical world. From navigating a complex facility to interpreting the needle on a pressure gauge, a robot’s “embodied reasoning” is what allows it to bridge the gap between digital intelligence and physical action.Today, we’re introducing Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, a significant upgrade to our reasoning-first model that enables robots to understand their environments with unprecedented precision. By enhancing spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding, we are bringing a new level of autonomy to the next generation of physical agents.This model specializes in reasoning capabilities critical for robotics, including visual and spatial understanding, task planning and success detection. It acts as the high-level reasoning model for a robot, capable of executing tasks by natively calling tools like Google Search to find information, vision-language-action models (VLAs) or any other third-party user-defined functions.Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 shows significant improvement over both Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 and Gemini 3.0 Flash, specifically enhancing spatial and physical reasoning capabilities such as pointing, counting, and success detection. We are also unlocking a new capability: instrument reading, enabling robots to read complex gauges and sight glasses — a use case we discovered through close collaboration with our partner, Boston Dynamics.Starting today, Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 is available to developers via the Gemini API and Google AI Studio. To help you get started, we are sharing a developer Colab containing examples of how to configure the model and prompt it for embodied reasoning tasks.Figure 1: Benchmark results comparing Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 with Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 and Gemini 3.0 Flash models. The instrument reading evaluations were run with agentic vision enabled (except for Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 which doesn’t support it). All other evals were run with agentic vision disabled. The single view and multiview success detection evaluations contain different examples so are not comparable.Pointing: The foundation of spatial reasoningPointing is a fundamental capability for an embodied reasoning model, evolving with each model generation. Points can be used to express many concepts, including:Spatial reasoning: Precision object detection and countingRelational logic: Making comparisons, such as identifying the smallest item in a set; defining "from-to" relationships (e.g move X to location Y)Motion reasoning: Mapping trajectories and identifying optimal grasp pointsConstraint compliance: Reasoning through complex prompts like "point to every object small enough to fit inside the blue cup"Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 can use points as intermediate steps to reason about more complex tasks. For example, it can use points to count items in an image, or to identify salient points on an image to help the model perform mathematical operations to improve its metric estimations.The example below shows Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6’s strengths in pointing to multiple elements, and knowing when and when not to point.Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 correctly identifies the number of hammers (2), scissors (1), paintbrushes (1), pliers (6), and a collection of garden tools which can be interpreted as a single group or multiple points. It does not point to requested items that are not present in the image — a wheelbarrow and Ryobi drill. In comparison Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 fails to identify the correct number of hammers or paint brushes, misses the scissors altogether, hallucinates a wheelbarrow and lacks precision on plier pointing. Gemini 3.0 Flash is close to Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, but does not handle the pliers as well.Success Detection: The engine of autonomyIn robotics, knowing when a task is finished is just as important as knowing how to start it. Success detection is a cornerstone of autonomy, serving as a critical decision-making engine that allows an agent to intelligently choose between retrying a failed attempt or progressing to the next stage of a plan.Achieving visual understanding in robotics is challenging, requiring sophisticated perception and reasoning capabilities combined with broad world knowledge in order to handle complicating factors such as occlusions, poor lighting and ambiguous instructions. Additionally, most modern robotics setups include multiple camera views such as an overhead and wrist-mounted feed. This means a system needs to understand how different viewpoints combine to form a coherent picture at each moment and across time.Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 advances multi-view reasoning, enabling the system to better understand multiple camera streams and the relationship between them, even in dynamic or occluded environments, as demonstrated in the typical multi-view scenario below.Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 takes cues from multiple camera views to determine when the task "put the blue pen into the black pen holder" is complete.Instrument reading: Real-world visual reasoningTo understand a key strength of Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, we must look at how it combines capabilities like spatial reasoning and world knowledge to solve complex, real-world problems. A perfect example is instrument reading.This task stems from facility inspection needs, a critical focus area for our partners at Boston Dynamics. Industrial facilities contain many instruments — thermometers, pressure gauges, chemical sight glasses and more — that require constant monitoring. Spot, a Boston Dynamics robot product, is able to visit the instruments throughout the facility and capture images of them.Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 enables robots to interpret a variety of instruments, including circular pressure gauges, vertical level indicators and modern digital readouts.Instrument reading requires complex visual reasoning. One must precisely perceive a variety of inputs — including the needles, liquid level, container boundaries, tick marks and more — and understand how they all relate to each other. In the case of sight glasses, this involves estimating how much the liquid fills the sightglass taking into account distortion from the camera perspective. Gauges typically have text describing the unit, which must be read and interpreted, and some have multiple needles referring to different decimal places that need to be combined.Capabilities like instrument reading and more reliable task reasoning will enable Spot to see, understand, and react to real-world challenges completely autonomously. Marco da Silva Vice President and General Manager of Spot at Boston Dynamics Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 achieves its highly accurate instrument readings by using agentic vision, which combines visual reasoning with code execution. The model takes intermediate steps: first zooming into an image to get a better read of small details in a gauge, then using pointing and code execution to estimate proportions and intervals and get an accurate reading, and ultimately applying its world knowledge to interpret meaning.Figure 2: How the different elements of Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 contribute to reaching a high level of performance on the instrument reading task.Read an analog gauge with accuracyThis example demonstrates how the model uses pointing and code execution for zooming to derive the reading of gauge down to sub tick accuracy.Our safest robotics model yetSafety is integrated into every level of our embodied reasoning models. Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 is our safest robotics model to date, demonstrating superior compliance with Gemini safety policies on adversarial spatial reasoning tasks compared to all previous generations.The model also shows a substantially improved capacity to adhere to physical safety constraints. For example, it makes safer decisions through spatial outputs like pointing regarding which objects can be safely manipulated under gripper or material constraints (e.g., “don't handle liquids”, “don't pick up objects heavier than 20kg“).We also tested how well the model identifies safety hazards in text and video scenarios based on real-life injury reports. On these tasks, our Gemini Robotics-ER models improve over baseline Gemini 3.0 Flash performance (+6% in text, +10% in video) in perceiving injury risks accurately.Figure 3: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 improves substantially compared to Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 on Safety Instruction Following which tests the ability to adhere to physical safety constraints. It improves compared to Gemini 3.0 Flash on pointing, and both models have very high accuracy for text. Gemini 3.0 Flash does better on bounding boxes.Collaborate with us to improve embodied reasoning for roboticsWe are committed to ensuring Gemini Robotics-ER provides maximum value to the robotics community. If current capabilities are limited for your specialized application, we invite you to submit this form with 10–50 labeled images illustrating specific failure modes to help us build more robust reasoning features. We look forward to collaborating with you to enhance these capabilities in our upcoming releases.Try Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 now on Google AI Studio

评论（默认中文）

sho_hn 2026/04/15 22:27
确实，通过使用类似大脑的编排模式，我们确实已经非常接近能够在现有的生成堆栈之上令人信服地模拟许多人类或至少是动物的行为……只要推理速度足够快，可以做更多的事情就好了。

这里的仪表读取示例很棒，但实际上，当然让系统综合 Python 脚本、运行 CV 任务、返回答案等目前相当慢。

一旦事情进展得更快，您还可以开始使用图像生成，让模型从拍摄的照片中推断出可能的未来，然后向自己描述它们并据此做出决策，就像这样的循环。我认为我们的假设是，在我们融入有意识的思维概念之前，我们的大脑会无意识地做类似的事情。

我真的很好奇如果我们有 100 倍或 1000 倍的推理吞吐量，我们可以构建什么东西。

It does all start to feel like we'd get fairly close to being able to convincingly emulate a lot of human or at least animal behavior on top of the existing generative stack, by using brain-like orchestration patterns ... if only inference was fast enough to do much more of it.

The gauge-reading example here is great, but in reality of course having the system synthesize that Python script, run the CV tasks, come back with the answer etc. is currently quite slow.

Once things go much faster, you can also start to use image generation to have models extrapolate possible futures from photos they take, and then describe them back to themselves and make decisions based on that, loops like this. I think the assumption is that our brains do similar things unconsciously, before we integrate into our conscious conception of mind.

I'm really curious what things we could build if we had 100x or 1000x inference throughput.

在 HN 中查看
moonu 2026/04/15 22:57
我不知道你是否已经看过这个，但 Taalas 做了一件有趣的事情，他们将模型直接嵌入到芯片上，这导致了超快的速度（https://chatjimmy.ai）但他们使用的模型是旧的小 Llama 模型，因此质量很差。但他们说它可以扩展，所以如果这是真的，那就太疯狂了，并解开了你所说的推论。
Idk if you've seen this already but Taalas does this interesting thing where they embed the model directly onto the chip, this leads to super-fast speeds (https://chatjimmy.ai) but the model they're using is an old small Llama model so the quality is pretty bad. But they say that it can scale, so if that's really true that'd be pretty insane and unlock the inference you're talking about.
在 HN 中查看
lachlan_gray 2026/04/15 23:44
当我看到这个版本时，我想到的正是机器人/控制系统！让我印象深刻的是实时前瞻搜索的可能性，有点像 alphazero 的 mcts。
Robotics/control systems is exactly what came to mind when I saw this release! What struck me is the possibility of look ahead search in real time, a bit like alphazero's mcts.
在 HN 中查看
pstuart 2026/04/16 08:58
这是一个令人着迷的提议，毫无疑问他们将在那里获得更大的模型，并且可能能够为大型 MOE 聚集多个模型。真正伟大的一件事是，如果他们能够降低功率要求——芯片需要 2.5KW，这对于大男孩使用的功率来说是适中的，但对于电池供电的机器人来说将是一个问题。
It's a fascinating proposition and no doubt they'll get bigger models in there, and likely be able to cluster multiple models for mega MOE. One thing that would really be great is if they could take the power requirements down -- the chip requires 2.5KW, which is modest in terms of what the big boys use but would be an issue on a battery powered robot.
在 HN 中查看
Kostic 2026/04/15 23:19
Taalas 表明，您可以通过将 LLM 转变为 ASIC 来加快 LLM 的速度，并生成超过 10k 的代币。现在只是时间问题了。
Taalas showed that you could make LLMs faster by turning them into ASICs and get 10k+ token generation. It's a matter of time now.
在 HN 中查看
timmg 2026/04/16 00:11
实际上想想很有趣：几年后，你可能会购买一个树莓派风格的计算机板，上面有一个额外的芯片，带有这些类型的实施模型之一，你可以将它放在流动站或其他东西中。
Actually pretty interesting to think: in a few years you might buy a raspberry pi style computer board with an extra chip on it with one of these types of embodiment models and you can slap it in a rover or something.
在 HN 中查看
tootie 2026/04/16 00:13
模仿人类行为真的是一个有价值的最终目标吗？人类作为进化的终点而存在，是在狩猎大型祈祷和有机工具制造方面精疲力尽。在过去的 100 年里，我们已经构建了大量的工业和住宅自动化工具，但没有一个是人形的。我想象一个家庭机器人管家会更像 R2D2，有很多很多的手臂。
Is emulating human behavior really a valuable end goal though? Humans exist as the evolutionary endpoint of exhaustion hunting large pray and organic tool-making. We've built loads of industrial and residential automation tools in the last 100 years and none of them are humanoid. I'd imagine a household robot butler would be more like R2D2 with lots and lots of arms.
在 HN 中查看
nozzlegear 2026/04/16 07:38
> 人类作为进化终点而存在

只是想迂腐地指出，我们还没有到达进化的终点。人类还在进化！

> Humans exist as the evolutionary endpoint

Just want to pedantically point out that we're not at our evolutionary endpoint yet. Humans are still evolving!

在 HN 中查看
hootz 2026/04/16 01:47
这是世界与我们互动的时候。如果机器人不模仿我们，我们就不能在所有事情上使用机器人，因为我们必须为非类人机器人调整一切。
It is when the world was made to interface with us. We can't use robots for everything if they aren't emulating us, because we would have to adapt everything for the non-humanlike robots.
在 HN 中查看
tootie 2026/04/16 05:37
我们在人类形态的限制下建造我们的生活空间，但这仍然并不意味着人类形态对于任何事物来说都是最佳的。在光滑表面上行驶的机器人没有理由应该用腿来代替轮子或踏板。没有理由有头。某种类型的手臂是常见的设计特征，但肯定没有理由有两个。没有理由对称。家用机器人可能会受到尺寸的限制（即在柜台高度看东西），但不会受到形状的限制。
We build our living spaces against the constraints of the human form, but that still doesn't imply the human form is optimal for anything. There's no reason a robot traveling over smooth surface should have legs instead of wheels or treads. There's no reason to have a head. Some kind of arm is a common design feature, but certainly no reason to have two. No reason to be symmetrical. A domestic robot may be constrained in terms of scale (ie see things at counter height) but not shape.
在 HN 中查看
famouswaffles 2026/04/16 09:42
>我们在人类形态的限制下建造我们的生活空间，但这仍然并不意味着人类形态对于任何事物来说都是最佳的。

我们在人类形态的限制下建造几乎所有我们期望与之互动的东西，而不仅仅是生活空间。是的，我们因为我们为人体建造了这些空间，人体显然是最佳选择。

>在光滑表面上行驶的机器人没有理由应该有腿而不是轮子或踏板。

这是有原因的。对于任何不光滑的表面，机器人都会变得毫无用处。楼梯有什么用呢？你不会为我们制定一个比“有效的脚”更好的定制解决方案。您认为针对每种情况构建一百万个不同的复杂机器人身体更好吗？这违背了通用目的的目的。

>We build our living spaces against the constraints of the human form, but that still doesn't imply the human form is optimal for anything.

We build just about everything we expect to interact with against the constraints of the human form, not just living spaces. And yes we because we built those spaces for the human body, the human body is by definition the optimal choice.

>There's no reason a robot traveling over smooth surface should have legs instead of wheels or treads.

There's a reason. The robot becomes useless for any surface that isn't smooth. What's it going to do about stairs ? You're not going to make a bespoke solution that generalizes for us better than 'feet that work'. Do you think it's better to built a million different complex robot bodies for every situation ? That defeats the purpose of being general purpose.

在 HN 中查看
hgoel 2026/04/16 06:06
实际上，要求机器人以可预测的方式移动（如果某个东西看起来像手臂，它应该像手臂一样移动，等等），并且有足够的力量来完成困难/累人的任务，同时如果出现问题也不会造成危险。
Really, the requirements are for the robot to move in predictable ways (if something looks like an arm, it ought to move like an arm, etc), and to have enough strength to be useful for difficult/tiring tasks while somehow also not being dangerous if something does go wrong.
在 HN 中查看
Glemllksdf 2026/04/16 02:30
每一个行为？当然不是，但除此之外，我们是一个非常非常漫长的进化的结果，我们周围没有其他东西比我们更聪明、更可调。

例如，通过模拟进行提前规划似乎是基于神经网络的架构中的一个非常好的工具。

Every single behavior? For sure not but otherwise we are the result of a very very long evolution and there is nothing else around us as smart and as adjustable.

The planing ahead thing through simulation for example seems to be a very good tool in neuronal network based architectures.

在 HN 中查看
LetsGetTechnicl 2026/04/16 00:04
如果我们将 slop 图像放入 slop 机器并取出 slop^2 会怎么样？
What if we put slop images into slop machines and got slop^2 back out
在 HN 中查看
vibe42 2026/04/16 00:09
一块土地。

一些机器人腿和手臂，大电池，现成的GPU。太阳能电池板。

提示：“在其限度内照顾好这片土地并种植一些蔬菜。”

A parcel of land.

A few robot legs and arms, big battery, off-the-shelf GPU. Solar panels.

Prompt: "Take care of all this land within its limits and grow some veggies."

在 HN 中查看
rcoveson 2026/04/16 05:32
至于其效果如何，尚无定论，但传统的提示是：“多结果子，繁衍生息”
Jury is still out as to how well it works, but the traditional prompt is: "Be fruitful, and multiply"
在 HN 中查看
prossercj 2026/04/16 08:08
不要忘记关于不要吃善恶知识树上的额外说明。
Don't forget the extra instructions about not eating from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
在 HN 中查看
culi 2026/04/16 00:37
如果事实证明“照顾这片土地”意味着加州照顾传统方式，定期进行小规模缓慢燃烧，该怎么办？经过一万多年的这种管理，许多重要的本地物种在没有灰烬的情况下甚至不会发芽。

或者它可能看起来像 satayoma（日本农民森林），或者它可能更类似于中非许多地区传统上实行的作物轮作，那里的根很重要。

在苏联向农民强行实施“现代科学农业”以实现现代化之前，俄罗斯实行的是等高耕作（在土地轮廓上套种成排作物以减缓水流流速）和马斯林耕作（在同一块土地上混合多种小麦和大麦）。现在，等高农业是一个活跃的研究领域，因为它们能够防止表土流失和建立土壤健康，而马斯林则提供卓越的产量稳定性，并且几乎不使用农药。

这还不包括我们记录的超过 40-120,000 种水稻品种。其中大多数都非常适应特定的地点——通常甚至是一个村庄。

我的观点是，没有一种方法可以照顾一块土地。这与土地本身的非生物特征之外的许多因素有关。您的目标和意图很重要，您总会找到本地化的独特适应方案。

What if it turns out that "take care of this land" means the traditional way California was taken care of with regular small slow burns. After over 10k years of this type of management there are many important native species that won't even germinate without the presence of ash.

Or it could turn out to look like satayoma (Japanese peasant forests) or it could be more similar to the crop rotation that was traditionally practiced in many parts of Central Africa where roots were important.

In Russia before the Soviets forced "modern scientific agriculture" on peasants to modernize, they practiced things like contour farming (where they interplanted rows of crops against the contours of the land to slow water down) and maslins (where they intermixed multiple varieties of wheat and barleys in the same patch). Now contour farming are an active area of research for their ability to prevent topsoil loss and build soil health while maslins provide superior yield stability and use little to no pesticides.

That's not even getting into the over 40-120,000 varieties of rice we've documented. Most of which are hyper adapted to a very specific location—often even a single village.

My point is there is no one way to take care of a plot of land. It's all relative to a number of factors beyond just the abiotic characteristics of the land itself. Your goals and intentions matter and you will always find localized unique adaptations.

在 HN 中查看
jayd16 2026/04/16 00:21
是的，我不确定目前进展如何。 https://proofofcorn.com/
Yeah I'm not sure how that's currently working out. https://proofofcorn.com/
在 HN 中查看
jerf 2026/04/16 00:53
你是说失败了？从该页面来看，我并没有明显看出有什么特别的问题。我认为没有人愚蠢到声称人工智能解决了“爱荷华州由于冬季条件仍然无法种植”的问题。
Are you saying that has failed? It isn't obvious to me from that page that anything in particular is going wrong. I don't think anyone is daft enough to claim that AI solves the "Iowa remains unplantable due to winter conditions" problem.
在 HN 中查看
jayd16 2026/04/16 01:25
从时间线来看，他们似乎无可救药地落后了。 目前是种植窗口期，他们没有土地，也没有人来种植。
Looking at the timeline they seem hopelessly behind. Its currently the planting window and they don't have land or a person to work it.
在 HN 中查看
jerf 2026/04/16 01:29
啊，谢谢你。我现在在爱荷华州北部，现在还不是播种窗口，所以我不确定他们在哪里。
Ah, thank you. It's not the planting window yet where I am, north of Iowa, so I wasn't certain where they were.
在 HN 中查看
adelie 2026/04/16 01:19
日志显示，在农场工人的介绍中，它已经“严重失败”和“被封锁了 68 天”，尽管日志回溯得不够远（并且过早切断），无法真正说明发生了什么。 https://proofofcorn.com/log
logs suggest it's been 'critically failing' and 'blocked for 68 days' on farmhand introduction, although the logs don't go back far enough (and cut off too early) to really tell what's going on. https://proofofcorn.com/log
在 HN 中查看
jonas21 2026/04/16 00:36
这是一种相反的问题——代理没有机器人手臂、腿或一块土地。它必须依靠人们才能获得土地、种植和收获玉米，而这些人却忽视了它。
That's kind of the opposite problem -- the agent doesn't have robot arms or legs or a parcel of land. It has to rely on people to get access to land and plant and harvest the corn, and those people are ignoring it.

harrall 2026/04/16 01:00

现代汽车现在拥有波士顿动力公司，并正在努力将机器人引入他们的工厂。

Google and Boston Dynamics (of Spot, Atlas fame) formed a partnership a while back and they’ve been working on building models together.

Hyundai now owns Boston Dynamics and is pushing to get the robots into their factories.

colinator 2026/04/16 01:43

Glemllksdf 2026/04/16 02:35
真的很不幸，我忘记了两周前我在 YT 上看到的有关此的视频。

这是关于 Google PaLM-E 的演变和进步。它基本上有两个模型，一个控制机器人，另一个是 llm，它们在某个注意力层中组合在一起。

Really unfortunate that I forgot what YT video i saw about this just 2 weeks ago.

It was about Googles PaLM-E evolution and progress. It basically has two models one which controls the robot, the other is a llm and they are combined together in some attention layer.

在 HN 中查看
johntb86 2026/04/16 03:34
一定是 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2mrGMMmrVNE 来自韦尔奇实验室。
Must have been https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2mrGMMmrVNE from Welch Labs.
在 HN 中查看
colinator 2026/04/16 04:29
这个视频很好看，谢谢你找到了。我基本上打赌，视频中描述的早期被放弃的方法“代码即策略”将击败其他所有方法。它不需要训练数据，并且可以立即推广到所有机器人。
That video is pretty good, thanks for finding it. I'm basically betting that an earlier, abandoned approach described in the video, "Code as Policy", will beat everything else. It requires no training data, and generalizes instantly to all robots.
在 HN 中查看
shireboy 2026/04/16 03:49
也许是愚蠢的问题：用例之一是模拟仪器的仪器读取。 我的大脑立即想到“这应该有一些传感器发送数据，而不是模拟”。 在某些情况下，让机器狗读取模拟传感器真的更合适吗？
Maybe dumb question: One of the use cases is instrument reading of analog instruments. My brain immediately goes to "this should have some sensor sending data, and not be analog". Is having a robot dog read analog sensors really a better fit in some cases?
在 HN 中查看
heisenzombie 2026/04/16 05:12
值得认真思考替代方案：

因此，我们将让一些工程师根据流程/环境/安全要求指定合适的数字替代品。我们将采购这些设备（注意到工业数字压力传感器可以轻松推升至 1 万美元），安排工厂停工（这要花多少钱？），然后付钱给管道安装工/锅炉制造商，用新的压力传感器更换旧压力表（您需要动火作业许可证吗？您让工程师签字了吗？）。然后，您的控制 Sparky 必须找到一种方法将分站路由回编组柜，以连接到现场总线/HART/modbus/任何网络（您有其中一个吗？），以便您的 SCADA 系统可以与其通信（您有其中一个吗？）。

显然，这并不是真正的同类比较，但我认为在工业环境中进行“简单”改变所涉及的成本很容易被严重低估。

It bears really thinking through the alternative:

So we're going to have some engineers specify suitable digital replacements given the process/environment/safety requirements. We'll procure those (noting that an industrial digital pressure transducer can easily push up towards $10k), schedule a plant shutdown (how much does that cost?), then pay a pipefitter/boilermaker to replace the old gauges with new pressure transmitters (do you need a hot work permit for that? Did you get your engineer to sign that off?). Then, your controls sparky has to find a way to route a drop back to your marshalling cabinet for connection into your fieldbus/HART/modbus/whatever network (do you have one of those?) so that your SCADA system can talk to it (do you have one of those?).

Obviously it's not really an apples-to-apples comparison, but I think the costs involved with making "simple" changes in industrial settings are easy to wildly underestimate.

在 HN 中查看
schainks 2026/04/16 05:39
我看到很多情况下，安装物联网相机比“关闭设备以拔下模拟仪器，连接数字仪器，校准它，然后重新启动设备”更可靠且成本更低。

如果它没有坏，就不要修理它——用一个廉价的相机对准它并进行一些云计算就足够了。

I can see many cases where installing an IoT camera will be more reliable and less costly than, "shut down equipment to unplug this analog instrument, hook up a digital one, calibrate it, then restart the equipment".

If it ain't broke don't fix it — pointing a cheap camera at it with some cloud compute will suffice.

在 HN 中查看
vessenes 2026/04/16 00:08
好的。但我找不到我最感兴趣的部分，即延迟。对于某些识别任务，这击败了他们的前沿视觉模型——对于机器人模型，我对赫兹感兴趣。由于这是一个“具体推理”模型，我假设它相当慢 - 它旨在与机器人上更快的循环模型相匹配。

无论如何，很酷。

Nice. I couldn't find the part that I'm most interested in though, latency. This beats their frontier vision model for some identification tasks -- for a robotics model, I'm interested in hz. Since this is an "Embodied Reasoning" model, I'm assuming it's fairly slow - it's designed to match with on-robot faster cycle models.

Anyway, cool.

在 HN 中查看
WarmWash 2026/04/16 00:14
在我在 AI studio 上进行的快速图像识别测试中，它的性能看起来与 3.1 pro 类似，但速度要快得多。它会“思考”，但只有几秒钟。

当然，这是为了计算动物的腿数，同时提供坐标和读取模拟时钟。不编码或解决难题。我想这个模型的模型权重的图像性能非常高。

In my quick image recognition testing on AI studio, it's performance seems similar to 3.1 pro, but is much much faster. It "thinks" but only for a few seconds.

Of course this is for counting animal legs while giving coordinates and reading analog clocks. Not coding or or solving puzzles. I imagine the image performance to model weight of this model is very high.

在 HN 中查看
vessenes 2026/04/16 01:21
我认为整个关于指向的重要性的讨论非常有趣。有人告诉我，虽然我不知道这是否属实，但狗是唯一能理解人类指点的动物。令人着迷的是，这可能是世界情报需求的基础。嗯，这是必需的，但有趣的是，认为它可能是必需的核心结构，或者学习它可能会强制形成某种有用的神经架构。

而且，我很失望地发现指向只是给出 x,y 坐标。我想看到机器人指着东西。

I thought the entire discourse on how important pointing is to be super interesting. I've been told, although I don't know if this is true, that dogs are the only animal that can understand human pointing. Fascinating to think this might be fundamental to world intelligence requirements. Well, it's required, but it's interesting to think that it might be a core structure required or that learning it might force some sort of neural architecture that's helpful.

And, I was disappointed to see that pointing was just giving x,y coords. I wanted to see robots pointing at stuff.

在 HN 中查看
gallerdude 2026/04/15 22:50
我最近一直在考虑人工智能机器人……如果在实验室内部他们有一个 GPT-2、GPT-3 机器人“等效”，你就不能真正发布它。如果机器人为你的洗碗机卸货时弄坏了你的一个盘子，这就是一次巨大的失败。

因此，幕后可能会取得令人惊叹的进展，只是还没有为公众做好准备。

I’ve been thinking about AI robotics lately… if internally at labs they have a GPT-2, GPT-3 “equivalent” for robotics, you can’t really release that. If a robot unloading your dishwasher breaks one of your dishes once, this is a massive failure.

So there might be awesome progress behind the scenes, just not ready for the general public.

在 HN 中查看
monkeydust 2026/04/15 23:10
周末我和罗宾·威廉姆斯一起看了《二百周年纪念日》（Bicentennial Man，1999）。如果你还没有看过，我认为这是一部很好、很及时的作品，而且适合儿童观看。不泄露剧情，卸载洗碗机的场景……拿走我的钱！
I ended up watching Bicentennial Man (1999) with Robin Williams over the weekend. If you haven't seen I thought it was a good and timely thing to watch and is kid friendly. Without giving away the plot, the scene where it was unloading the dishwasher...take my money!
在 HN 中查看
NitpickLawyer 2026/04/15 23:55
> 如果机器人为您的洗碗机卸货时损坏了您的一个盘子，这就是一次巨大的失败。

这有点夸张了，不是吗？早期的扫地机器人会被袜子缠住，把宠物粪便拖得满地都是，打碎玻璃等等，但市场接受了这一点，并不断发展，现在我们有很多来自不同公司的清洁机器人，包括来自中国的廉价间谍机器人。

事实上，我认为成为第一个将机器人部署到家庭中的人有很多价值，即使它们并不完美。您收集的数据量是无价的，而且从表面上看，无法在实验室中合成生成。

我认为“更安全”的选择仍然是“首先将它们带到工厂，然后是办公室，最后是家庭”，但无论如何我确信有人会直接跳到家庭部署。

> If a robot unloading your dishwasher breaks one of your dishes once, this is a massive failure.

That's a bit exaggerated, no? Early roombas would get tangled in socks, drag pet poop all over the floor, break glass stuff and so on, and yet the market accepted that, evolved, and now we have plenty of cleaning robots from various companies, including cheap spying ones from china.

I actually think that there's a lot of value in being the first to deploy bots into homes, even if they aren't perfect. The amount of data you'd collect is invaluable, and by the looks of it, can't be synth generated in a lab.

I think the "safer" option is still the "bring them to factories first, offices next and homes last", but anyway I'm sure someone will jump straight to home deployments.

在 HN 中查看
doubled112 2026/04/16 00:09
我在洗碗机中装卸时弄坏了盘子。 我是不是一个巨大的失败？

我的非人工智能洗碗机甚至不能一直把水留在里面。 没有什么是完美的。

I have broken dishes loading and unloading the dishwasher. Am I a massive failure?

My non-AI dishwasher can't even always keep the water inside. Nothing is perfect.

在 HN 中查看
Rekindle8090 2026/04/16 00:27
如果有人付了 100 英镑让你装卸洗碗机，而为了做到这一点而进行的研究花费了数千亿美元、数十年的研究、数十万的研究人员，而这是你唯一能做的事情，是的，你将是一个巨大的失败。
If someone paid 100 grand for you to load and unload the the dishwasher, and the research to be able to do it costed hundreds of billions, decades of research, hundreds of thousands of researchers, and that was the ONLY thing you could do, yes, you WOULD be a massive failure.
在 HN 中查看
logicprog 2026/04/16 01:05
> 如果机器人为您的洗碗机卸货时损坏了您的一个盘子，这就是一次巨大的失败。

根据打碎​​盘子的频率，这对我这个人来说将是一个巨大的进步，因为我打碎了一个非常重要的盘子，我平均每月需要使用约 2 次。

> If a robot unloading your dishwasher breaks one of your dishes once, this is a massive failure.

Depending on what the rate of breaking dishes is, this would be a massive improvement on me, a human being, since I break a really important dish I needed to use like ~2x per month on average.

在 HN 中查看
nancyminusone 2026/04/16 03:02
你真的每两周打破一次盘子吗？这看起来异常笨拙。

郑重声明，我不是来羞辱你的。

You really break a dish once every 2 weeks? That seems exceptionally clumsy.

Not here to shame you for it, for the record.

在 HN 中查看
logicprog 2026/04/16 04:29

这就是我 ;_;

> That seems exceptionally clumsy

在 HN 中查看
adityamwagh 2026/04/16 03:46
机器人技术没有足够的互联网规模数据。差距是巨大的！因此，任何声称拥有类似 GPT 模型的人都是不诚实的。
There's not enough internet-scale data for robotics. The gap is huge! So anyone that claims to have a GPT like model is not behing honest.
在 HN 中查看
Glemllksdf 2026/04/16 02:38
从经济角度来看，该行业无论如何都是迄今为止最相关的。它更容易，因为环境更受控制，专业人员配置和维护机器人，他们批量购买并且有更多的钱。

我对家用机器人的关心不是洗碗机，而是电视屏幕、玻璃门、玻璃桌子、动物（鱼/水族馆）等，机器人可能会走过、触摸或跌倒。

From an economic standpoint the industry is anyway the most relevant by far. Its easier as the env is a lot more controlled, professionals configure and maintain the robots, they buy in bulk and have more money.

My concern with a household robot is not the dishwasher but the tv screen, the glas door, glas table, animals (fish/aquarium) etc. the robot might walk through, touch through or fall onto.

在 HN 中查看
spwa4 2026/04/15 23:53
它被称为“VLA”（视觉-语言-动作）模型： https://huggingface.co/models?pipeline_tag=robotics

VLA 模型本质上是采用网络摄像头屏幕截图 + 一些文本（认为“将红色块放在右侧框中”）并输出电机控制指令来实现这一点。

注意：“Gemini Robotics-ER”不是 VLA，尽管 Gemini 也有一个 VLA 模型：“Gemini Robotics”。

演示： https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DeBLc2D6bvg

It's called "VLA" (vision-language-action) models: https://huggingface.co/models?pipeline_tag=robotics

VLA models essentially take a webcam screenshot + some text (think "put the red block in the right box") and output motor control instructions to achieve that.

Note: "Gemini Robotics-ER" is not a VLA, though Gemini does have a VLA model too: "Gemini Robotics".

A demo: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DeBLc2D6bvg

在 HN 中查看
Isamu 2026/04/16 03:12
>我们迄今为止最安全的机器人模型 安全性已融入到我们具身推理模型的各个层面。 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 是我们迄今为止最安全的机器人模型，与前几代产品相比，在对抗性空间推理任务上表现出了对 Gemini 安全政策的卓越合规性。

安全指南很有趣，他们将其视为他们渴望实现的目标，这似乎很现实。黄金时段还没有完全准备好。

>Our safest robotics model yet Safety is integrated into every level of our embodied reasoning models. Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 is our safest robotics model to date, demonstrating superior compliance with Gemini safety policies on adversarial spatial reasoning tasks compared to all previous generations.

The safety guidelines are interesting, they treat them as a goal that they are aspiring to achieve, which seems realistic. It’s not quite ready for prime time yet.

在 HN 中查看
w10-1 2026/04/16 01:25
这种方法会破坏对基于物理或基于建模的推理的关键投资吗？

我完全支持基于相关点的任务推理和多视图识别。 我对松散的世界“理解”感到非常不舒服。

我看到的错误模型是，例如，这种“视觉理解”将使事情大部分正确：足以构建甚至交付产品。 然而，这些只是基于训练集的概率保证，并且这些保证不太可能在与复杂的交互世界的接触中幸存下来，特别是因为机器人经常随着任务的变化而改变用途。

因此，这是一种道德产品风险：它提供了初步结果，但将风险推迟到以后，因此产品开发人员将有动力去开发并让用户承担责任。 （事实上​​：无论如何，用户都要对集成风险负责。）

它破坏了我们的假设：我们认为你可以采用 MVP 并将其产品化，但在这种情况下，你永远无法以可靠的方式反向拟合模型以符合物理原理。 我怀疑是否有任何方法可以利用双子座来依赖物理模型，因此我们最终将在市场上得到大部分有效的沉没投资——垃圾机器人如此便宜，以至于紧凑的机器人无法生存。

Would this approach destroy critical investments in physics- or modeling-based reasoning?

I'm all for the task reasoning and the multi-view recognition, based on relevant points. I'm very uncomfortable with the loose world "understanding".

The fault model I see is that e.g., this "visual understanding" will get things mostly right: enough to build and even deliver products. However, these are only probabilistic guarantees based on training sets, and those are unlikely to survive contact with a complex interactive world, particularly since robots are often repurposed as tasks change.

So it's a kind of moral-product-hazard: it delivers initial results but delays risk to later, so product developers will have incentives to build and leave users holding the bag. (Indeed: users are responsible for integration risks anyway.)

It hacks our assumptions: we think that you can take an MVP and productize it, but in this case, you'll never backfit the model to conform to the physics in a reliable way. I doubt there's any way to harness Gemini to depend on a physics model, so we'll end up with mostly-working sunk investments out in the market - slop robots so cheap that tight ones can't survive.

pants2 2026/04/16 02:02

I wonder what new laws of physics AI models have discovered encoded opaquely in their weights, yet to be discovered by physicists

Lucasoato 2026/04/16 03:21
与此同时，今天下午我完全无法使用 Gemini 3.1 pro（两个月前发布），无论是 API 还是订阅。

Google 状态页面中没有任何报告，甚至 CLI 也没有响应，它只是在那里等待答案，即使在 10 分钟后也永远不会到达。

Meanwhile, gemini 3.1 pro (that was released two months ago) was completely unavailable to me this afternoon, neither with API nor Subscription.

Nothing was reported in Google status page, not even the CLI is responding, it’s just left there waiting for an answer that will never arrive even after 10 minutes.

colinator 2026/04/16 02:39
我也刚刚在市场上购买一个小型实验机器人。我得到了 hiwonder armpi-fpv。避免它，执行器非常糟糕 - 它们非常“磨蹭”，机器人在移动时会疯狂地抖动。 lekivi有这样的问题吗？
I was also just in the market for a small experiment robot. I got the hiwonder armpi-fpv. Avoid it, the actuators are pretty bad - they're very 'grindy', the robot jitters like crazy when it moves. Any such problems with the lekivi?

[deleted] 2026/04/15 23:48 deleted

[已删除]

[deleted]

在 HN 中查看
martythemaniak 2026/04/16 02:21
正如文章指出的那样，普通的 Gemini 和 Gemma 也具有空间推理能力，我决定通过看看 Gemini 是否可以成功驾驶小型漫游车来测试这一能力，它大部分都做到了： https://martin.drashkov.com/2026/02/letting-gemini-drive-my-...

法学硕士非常擅长机器人技术所缺少的任务：理解、推理、规划等，因此我们可能会在各种机器人应用中看到它们更多的使用。我想现在的主要问题是：

- 谁发送各种精细运动命令。大多数实验室/研究人员的答案是“更小的扩散模型”，因此法学硕士充当规划者，然后更小的更快的扩散模型控制实际的电机。我怀疑在很多情况下你可以通过相当于工具调用的方式来逃脱 - LLM 只是调用一个特定的子例程，例如“前进 1m”或“向右倾斜相机”

- 你对记忆做什么？所有模型要么纯粹是反应性的，要么采用很小的历史记录并将其用作输入的一部分，因此它们都需要某种类型的内存/状态管理系统来实际上允许它们在任务上工作一段时间以上。我不清楚这是否会被标准化并成为模型本身的一部分，或者每个人都会做自己的事情。

As the article notes regular Gemini and Gemma also have spatial reasoning capabilities, which I decided to test by seeing if Gemini could drive a little rover successfully, which it mostly did: https://martin.drashkov.com/2026/02/letting-gemini-drive-my-...

LLMs are really good at the sort of tasks that have been missing from robotics: understanding, reasoning, planning etc, so we'll likely see much more use of them in various robotics applications. I guess the main question right now is:

- who sends in the various fine-motor commands. The answer most labs/researchers have is "a smaller diffusion model", so the LLM acts as a planner, then a smaller faster diffusion model controls the actual motors. I suspect in many cases you can get away with the equivalent of a tool call - the LLM simply calls out a particular subroutine, like "go forward 1m" or "tilt camera right"

- what do you do about memory? All the models are either purely reactive or take a very small slice of history and use that as part of the input, so they all need some type of memory/state management system to actually allow them to work on a task for more than a little while. It's not clear to me whether this will be standardized and become part of models themselves, or everyone will just do their own thing.

在 HN 中查看
colinator 2026/04/16 02:29
对于精细运动命令：或者，模型可以编写代码来动态生成它们。在我非常有限的实验中，它似乎有效。

至于内存：我的方法是给机器人一个 python repl，基本上是一个文件系统 - LLM 可以编写模块，通过交互式 python 戳机器人等。

基本上，法学硕士成为一名机器人程序员，实时编写代码。

For the fine-motor commands: or, the model can write the code to generate them on the fly. It seems to work, in my very limited experiment.

As for memory: my approach is to give the robot a python repl and, basically, a file system - the LLM can write modules, poke at the robot via interactive python, etc.

Basically, the LLM becomes a robot programmer, writing code in real-time.

在 HN 中查看
jeffbee 2026/04/15 22:47
展示杀人狗使用价值 $$$ 的模型时间读取仪表并不是一个令人惊叹的演示。我们已经知道如何用机器视觉读取仪表。我们还知道如何以 50 美元以下的价格从工业目录中订购数字仪表。
Showing the murder dog reading a gauge using $$$ worth of model time is kinda not an amazing demo. We already know how to read gauges with machine vision. We also know how to order digital gauges out of industrial catalogs for under $50.
在 HN 中查看
readams 2026/04/15 23:53
我认为有趣的地方在于，你可以将这些机器人系统放入一个不一定是专门为处理它们而设置的环境中。仪表的 50 美元并不是真正的成本：它是工程时间来检查整个环境并进行设置，以便机器人系统可以处理每项特定任务，每项任务都需要一些定制设置。
I think that where this gets interesting is when you can just drop these robotic systems into an environment that wasn't necessarily set up specifically to handle them. The $50 for your gauge isn't really the cost: it's engineering time to go through the whole environment and set it up so that the robotic system can deal with each of the specific tasks, each of which will require some bespoke setup.
在 HN 中查看
snickmy 2026/04/15 23:30
同意。我不清楚这篇文章的亮点是什么。是模型的多模态（可以取代计算机视觉），是推理部分，还是使得在上面进行开发变得非常容易的整体包装器？
Agree. I'm unclear what's the highlight of this post. Is the multimodality of the model (that can replace computer vision), is it the reasoning part, is it the overall wrapper that makes it very easy to develop on top?
在 HN 中查看
PunchTornado 2026/04/16 02:07
事实上，这不是特定于任务的。
It's the fact that is not task specific.

BDPW 2026/04/16 02:21

Completely agree, I get that this is a stepping stone for future, more reliable robots but I found the demonstration underwhelming.
